item.title: Pershing Square Has Taken New Stake in Microsoft, Ackman Says
item.description: Pershing Square Chief Executive Officer Bill Ackman said he’s taken a new stake in Microsoft Corp. after a recent decline in the share price.

item.title: These Memory Stocks Are Getting Cheaper Despite Scorching Rally
item.description: Insatiable demand for memory chips has some of the industry’s top stocks defying the market’s math, becoming cheaper to own even as their share prices soar to nosebleed levels.

item.title: Dollar Rallies Toward Best Week Since March on Fed Hike View
item.description: The dollar is heading for its best week in two months after US data showed price pressures that could push the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates over the next year.

item.title: Ghana’s World-Leading Stock Rally Fuels Prospect of Bank IPOs
item.description: A world-leading rally on Ghana’s stock market and an improving economy are likely to encourage lenders to plan initial share sales, according to the head of the nation’s bourse.

item.title: ByteDance Stake Anchors $3 Billion Fund in Win for Investor HSG
item.description: HSG, the investment firm formerly known as Sequoia Capital China, has closed a $3 billion continuation vehicle anchored by a stake in ByteDance Ltd., according to people familiar with the matter. The move allowed some US investors to exit the highly-scrutinized asset.

item.title: Morgan Stanley Upbeat on South Africa Outlook Despite Oil Shock
item.description: Morgan Stanley remains bullish on South Africa’s longer-term reform trajectory even as a global oil shock challenges the economy, fans inflation and may trigger higher interest rates.

item.title: Thai PM Gathers Tycoons for Rare Economy Talk Amid Crisis Alarm
item.description: Thai Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul is set to host some of the country’s top billionaires and industry titans for a rare discussion on the economy, which is facing “a wave of crises” triggered by the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Technoprobe Shares Surge by a Record After Raising Guidance
item.description: Technoprobe SpA rose by a record amount after the Italian company raised its guidance and said demand is surging for its semiconductor testing equipment.

item.title: Why SocGen's Albert Edwards Sees Double-Digit Inflation Coming Back
item.description: A conversation with the famed uber-bear.

item.title: Odd Lots: Albert Edwards Believes Inflation Will Surge (Podcast)
item.description: Making a long career as a bear at a sell-side institution is tough. Generally financial markets have done quite well which means forecasting doom and gloom is, usually, only tenable for so long. Which is why we wanted to talk to one of the most successful bears out there. Société Générale has let Albert Edwards out of the bear cage for today’s episode. Edwards knows his reputation as a bear is well deserved: He believes, among other things, double-digit inflation is in the offing. We also talk a

item.title: China’s Biggest Courier Is Set to Open Gold Vault in Hong Kong
item.description: SF Holding Co., China’s biggest express-delivery firm, is set to open a gold vault in Hong Kong to tap demand for storage as the city pushes forward with plans to become a precious-metals hub.

item.title: How Iran War Put Inflation-Linked Bonds Back in Fashion
item.description: A surge in energy prices triggered by the Iran war is prompting investors to revisit a familiar trade: inflation-linked bonds.

item.title: China’s Multiple Economies Make Factory Dominance Hard to Crack
item.description: It’s not just China’s sheer size that makes competing against its manufacturers so hard — but also the nation’s vast disparity in development.

item.title: Gilts Slump as Investors Brace for Burnham Challenge to Starmer
item.description: Gilts tumbled after Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham secured a pathway to potentially challenge Keir Starmer for the UK prime minister’s job, threatening a fresh bout of political instability that investors fear could result in more expansive fiscal policy.

item.title: South Africa Evacuating Sub-Antarctic Base After Fuel Delay
item.description: South Africa said it’s evacuating researchers at a sub-Antarctic Marion Island base after the Iran war delayed fuel deliveries needed for the operations.

item.title: UAE to Complete New Hormuz-Bypass Oil Pipeline by 2027
item.description: The United Arab Emirates is building a new pipeline bypassing the Strait of Hormuz and plans to have it in operation by 2027.

item.title: Billionaire Motsepe-Backed Bank Makes All Employees Shareholders
item.description: GoTyme Bank Ltd. has invited all its employees to share ownership in the digital lender controlled by South African billionaire Patrice Motsepe as it seeks to scale up operations.

item.title: Bank of Korea’s New Board Member Flags Inflation, Housing Risks
item.description: The Bank of Korea’s newest board member highlighted mounting inflation and financial-stability risks tied to the Middle East conflict, housing prices and capital flows in his inaugural remarks Friday, reinforcing expectations the central bank is moving toward a hawkish bias.

item.title: Pakistan Uses Newfound Diplomatic Clout to Get Persian Gulf LNG
item.description: Pakistan has imported its second shipment of liquefied natural gas from the Persian Gulf in a week, showing how Islamabad is leveraging its newfound geopolitical influence to ease an energy crunch.

item.title: STT Global Data Centres Is Said to Plan $500 Million India IPO
item.description: STT Global Data Centres India Pvt. is considering an initial public offering in Mumbai that could raise as much as $500 million, according to people familiar with the matter, as investor demand for digital infrastructure accelerates.

item.title: Kioxia Prepares US Listing After Riding AI Boom to Record Profit
item.description: Kioxia Holdings Corp., the world’s best performing major stock this year, said it would list its shares in the US as it reaps the benefits of a global memory chip shortage that’s ratcheted up prices of the vital component to historic highs.

item.title: Mizuho Plans to Reduce Orico Stake After Activist Pressure
item.description: Mizuho Financial Group Inc. plans to trim its stake in Orient Corp., after an activist investor urged it to either consolidate or sell its holding in the credit card company.

item.title: Citi Says African Borrowers Are Turning Creative on Debt Sales
item.description: African governments are increasingly turning to unconventional borrowing strategies, as high global interest rates and volatile currencies keep many shut out of traditional dollar funding markets, according to Citigroup Inc.

item.title: UK Bonds Plunge on Burnham Concerns and Inflation Fears
Curious Yen Spikes Have Traders Gaming Out Japan ‘Warning Shots’
item.description: Every few trading sessions, the yen jolts higher against the dollar, and then it suddenly drops.

item.title: Robot Makers Rule Hottest Asia Stock Trades on Physical AI Boom
item.description: Robotics has become one of the hottest stock themes in Asia as the artificial intelligence trade broadens beyond the region’s chipmakers.

item.title: EM Stocks Tumble Most in More Than a Month as Oil Jumps
item.description: Emerging-market stocks and currencies declined after two consecutive sessions of gains, pressured by rising oil prices and a cautious mood among investors ahead of the weekend.

item.title: Remittance Growth Slows to Nearly Three-Year Low in Risk for Philippine Economy
item.description: Money sent home by Filipinos working overseas grew at the slowest pace in nearly three years in March, reflecting the impact of the global energy crisis sparked by the Iran war and signaling more weakness in the Philippine economy.

item.title: India’s RBI Lifts Bond-Trading Target for Primary Dealers by 48%
item.description: The Reserve Bank of India raised trading targets for the country’s bond market makers in a bid to boost liquidity, driving a surge in activity in the 10-year benchmark security, according to people familiar with the development.

item.title: China Skeptic to Lead Solomon Islands After Pact With Beijing
item.description: The Solomon Islands, a small Pacific nation caught in the China and US competition, elected a new prime minister seen as less friendly to Beijing than his predecessors.
